24 July 2025 Military News

News Reports

Current Operations

Defense Policy / Programs

  • Navy Aims to Harness Innovation, Modernize Fleet Capabilities DoD News 24 Jul 2025 -- Years of shrinking fleet size, diminished shipbuilding capacity and on-time repair delivery are persistent challenges for the Navy and the nation, said Navy Adm. Daryl L. Caudle, who spoke today at a Senate Armed Services Committee nomination hearing for his reappointment to admiral and to become chief of naval operations.
  • Air Force stands up A6 for warfighter communications, cyber systems US Air Force 24 Jul 2025 -- The Department of the Air Force has created a new AF/A6 Deputy Chief of Staff office dedicated to warfighter communications and cyber systems. This structure is designed to address operational communications and cyber needs effectively throughout the force.
  • USS Thomas Hudner Returns from Deployment to 4th and 6th Fleet US Navy 24 Jul 2025 -- The Arleigh Burke-class guided-missile destroyer USS Thomas Hudner (DDG 116) returned to Naval Station Mayport July 23, concluding a five-month deployment across multiple geographic theaters, including the U.S. 4th and 6th Fleet areas of operations.
  • Cyber Scholars Converge on Washington, Prepare to Defend the Nation's Digital Frontlines DoD News 24 Jul 2025 -- Fifty top university and college cyber scholars from nearly 30 states and U.S. territories gathered at George Washington University's Science and Engineering Hall for the Defense Department Cyber Service Academy boot camp, July 21-22, 2025.
  • USSF Space Systems Command stands up two new System Deltas United States Space Force 24 Jul 2025 -- Space Systems Command, the U.S. Space Force field command responsible for developing and delivering resilient space capabilities, stood up two System Deltas in an activation ceremony at Los Angeles Air Force Base on July 10.
  • Space Systems Command's CASR conducts second wargame United States Space Force 24 Jul 2025 -- Data from real-world scenarios set the stage for Space Systems Command's Commercial Augmentation Space Reserve Space Domain Awareness wargame. Wargames are strategy games designed to simulate real-world warfare scenarios to examine warfighting concepts and tactics, train officers in tactical and strategic decision-making and assess potential outcomes.
  • Final Environmental Impact Statement for the Enhanced Integrated Air and Missile Defense System on Guam Now Available MDA 25 Jul 2025 -- The Missile Defense Agency (MDA), in cooperation with the United States (U.S.) Air Force, U.S. Army, U.S. Navy, and the Federal Aviation Administration (FAA), has prepared a Final Environmental Impact Statement (EIS) for the Enhanced Integrated Air and Missile Defense (EIAMD) System on Guam.

  • Meeting on developing the Navy's submarine forces President of Russia 24 Jul 2025 -- During his working trip to the Archangelsk Region, the President chaired a meeting on the development strategy for the Russian Navy's submarine forces.
  • Flag raising ceremony on Knyaz Pozharsky nuclear-powered cruiser President of Russia 24 Jul 2025 -- Vladimir Putin took part in a ceremony to raise the naval flag on the Knyaz Pozharsky nuclear-powered submarine cruiser, which is entering service with the Navy, held at the Sevmash plant in Severodvinsk.
  • Meeting with Andrei Kostin and Andrei Puchkov President of Russia 24 Jul 2025 -- The President met with Board Chairman of the United Shipbuilding Corporation (USC), CEO of VTB Bank Andrei Kostin and the USC CEO Andrei Puchkov.
  • Navy's Submarine Forces Play Key Role in Ensuring Russia's Sovereignty - Putin Sputnik 24 Jul 2025 -- The Russian Navy's submarine forces play a key role in ensuring the country's sovereignty and security, Russian President Vladimir Putin said on Thursday.

  • Bradleys And HAWKs: What's In Washington's $322M Arms Sale To Ukraine RFE/RL 24 Jul 2025 -- After negotiations between Russia and Ukraine on July 23 ended without major progress, Washington announced up to $322 million in military sales to Kyiv.
  • Ukraine - M109 Self-Propelled Howitzer Maintenance, Repair, and Overhaul Capability DSCA 24 Jul 2025 -- The State Department has made a determination approving a possible Foreign Military Sale to the Government of Ukraine of M109 Self-Propelled Howitzer Maintenance, Repair, and Overhaul Capability and related equipment for an estimated cost of $150 million. The Defense Security Cooperation Agency delivered the required certification notifying Congress.
  • Ukraine - Air Defense Sustainment DSCA 24 Jul 2025 -- The State Department has made a determination approving a possible Foreign Military Sale to the Government of Ukraine of Air Defense Sustainment and related equipment for an estimated cost of $180 million. The Defense Security Cooperation Agency delivered the required certification notifying Congress.
  • US Approves Potential $172Mln Sale to Ukraine Sputnik 24 Jul 2025 -- The United States has approved $172 million for Ukraine to maintain and repair HAWK air defense systems, the Defense Security Cooperation Agency said on Wednesday.
  • US Approves Potential Sale Of $322 Million In Arms To Ukraine After Little Progress At Istanbul Talks RFE/RL 24 Jul 2025 -- The United States on July 23 announced the approval of $322 million in arms sales to bolster Ukraine's air defenses and its armored combat vehicles.
  • Denys Shmyhal: We anticipate the EU's involvement in financing joint initiatives aimed at advancing defence innovations Ukraine MOD 24 Jul 2025
  • Denys Shmyhal discussed military support for Ukraine with the Minister of Defence of Norway Ukraine MOD 24 Jul 2025
  • Ukraine and the Netherlands held discussions on developing aviation, investing in drone production, and strengthening air defense capabilities Ukraine MOD 24 Jul 2025
  • Denys Shmyhal: The Government launches a grant program to scale up the production of explosives Ukraine MOD 24 Jul 2025
  • Denys Shmyhal addresses military support for Ukraine with Norwegian Defence Minister Ukraine Government 24 Jul 2025 -- Minister of Defence of Ukraine Denys Shmyhal held a discussion with Norway's Minister of Defence, Tore Sandvik.
  • Ministry of Digital Transformation: Brave1 to issue grants for explosives production Ukraine Government 24 Jul 2025 -- Manufacturers of explosives will be able to receive support to scale up production. The Government has adopted a resolution to launch a dedicated grant competition through the Brave1 defence-tech cluster.

  • Taiwan, Somaliland sign coast guard cooperation deal CNA 24 Jul 2025 -- President Lai Ching-te (賴清德) on Thursday lauded a new agreement signed between Taiwan and Somaliland in Taipei that day, saying it would pave the way for new maritime cooperation between the two sides.

  • Respected Comrade Kim Jong Un Oversees Firing Contest of Artillery Sub-units of Large Combined Units of KPA KCNA 24 Jul 2025 -- A firing contest among artillery sub-units of large combined units of the Korean People's Army was conducted on July 23 amid the hot wind of training to further consolidate the militant might of the elite revolutionary army.
  • Top DPRK leader oversees artillery firing contest People's Daily 24 Jul 2025 -- Kim Jong Un, top leader of the Democratic People's Republic of Korea (DPRK), oversaw on Wednesday a firing contest involving artillery sub-units of large combined units of the Korean People's Army, the official Korean Central News Agency reported Thursday.

  • Accelerating the acquisition of drone and counter drone technology Australia DOD 24 Jul 2025 -- The Albanese Government is accelerating the acquisition of cutting-edge drone and counter-drone technologies for the Australian Defence Force (ADF) to keep Australian's safe and protect our military assets.
  • Boarding teams build partnerships Australia DOD 24 Jul 2025 -- If someone hears those words shouted with authority while a Royal Australian Navy (RAN) boarding party climbs on to their vessel, they'd better hope they aren't harbouring contraband, as security units like these are serious business.
  • Talisman Sabre's new advantage takes shape Australia DOD 24 Jul 2025 -- Amid the backrooms of Exercise Talisman Sabre 2025, a significant first occurrence is highlighting the trust between the exercise partners.
  • Tanks on the Hook for Talisman Sabre Australia DOD 24 Jul 2025 -- Four of the Australian Army's new M1A2 SEPv3 Abrams main battle tanks and a MA88 Recovery Vehicle have been craned into the tight confines of the United States landing craft USAV Paulus Hook in a global first for interoperability.
  • Brewing new warfighting tactics in the lab Australia DOD 24 Jul 2025 -- Holding one of the key positions within the Australian Army's rapid transformation into a contemporary, integrated and adaptive fighting force is Battle Laboratory, or Battle Lab.
  • Sister ships sail side by side Australia DOD 24 Jul 2025 -- If HMAS Sydney looked like it was sailing with a twin, that's because it was. The Royal Australian Navy's guided missile destroyer sailed with Spain's ESPS Méndez Núñez as part of the United Kingdom's Carrier Strike Group deployment.

  • AUKUS treaty deepens UK-Australia defence partnership to generate £20 billion in trade and create 7,000 new jobs UK MOD 24 Jul 2025 -- Foreign Secretary and Defence Secretary in Australia alongside UK's Carrier Strike Group - demonstrating government's commitment to a free and open Indo-Pacific.

  • Royal Canadian Navy to Pay Off Kingston-class vessels Canada 24 Jul 2025 -- In the fall of 2025, the Royal Canadian Navy (RCN) will begin paying off the Kingston-class Maritime Coastal Defence Vessels (MCDVs).

  • Inaugural Meeting of the Pakistan-Türkiye Joint Standing Committee (JSC) on Security, Defence and Intelligence Pakistan Ministry of Foreign Affairs 24 Jul 2025 -- The inaugural meeting of the Pakistan-Türkiye Joint Standing Committee (JSC) on Security, Defence and Intelligence was held today in Islamabad. The Turkish delegation was led by Director General for South Asia, Ambassador Cihad Erginay, and the Pakistani side was headed by Additional Secretary (Afghanistan & West Asia), Ministry of Foreign Affairs, Ambassador Syed Ali Asad Gillani.
  • Vice Admiral Mohammed bin Abdulrahman Al Ghuraybi, Chief of the Naval Staff, Royal Saudi Naval Forces called on General Sahir Shamshad Mirza, NI (M), Chairman Joint Chiefs of Staff Committee (CJCSC) at Joint Staff Headquarters, Rawalpindi. ISPR Pakistan 24 Jul 2025

  • Egypt - National Advanced Surface-to-Air Missile System DSCA 24 Jul 2025 -- The State Department has made a determination approving a possible Foreign Military Sale to the Government of Egypt of National Advanced Surface-to-Air Missile System and related elements of logistics and program support for an estimated cost of $4.67 billion. The Defense Security Cooperation Agency delivered the required certification notifying Congress of this possible sale today.

  • Following Operation Rising Lion Achievements: Israel MOD Director General Conducts First Working Visit to India Israel MOD 24 Jul 2025 -- Building on the success of Operation Rising Lion, Israel Ministry of Defense (IMOD) Director General, Maj. Gen. (Res.) Amir Baram, conducted his first working visit to India this week to strengthen defense cooperation between the two nations.

  • NZ Army soldiers link up with Aussies for amphibious training New Zealand Defence Force 24 Jul 2025 -- Boots and heavy vehicles hit the shores of Queensland's Cowley Beach, kicking off the first phase of Exercise Sea Master - a littoral amphibious training exercise running concurrently with Talisman Sabre 2025.

  • More than 40 PAF aircraft on standby for possible relief ops Philippine News Agency 24 Jul 2025 -- The Philippine Air Force (PAF) said Thursday it has more than 40 aircraft on standby for possible relief missions amid the severe weather caused by the enhanced southwest monsoon and a series of tropical cyclones.

  • Secretary Rubio's Call with Portuguese Foreign Minister Rangel US Dept. of State 24 Jul 2025
  • Secretary Rubio's Meeting with Austrian Foreign Minister Meinl-Reisinger US Dept. of State 24 Jul 2025
  • The United States Welcomes Senegal's Signing of the Artemis Accords US Dept. of State 24 Jul 2025 -- Senegal became the 56th signatory of the Artemis Accords at NASA HQ today. Director General of the Senegalese Space Studies Agency Maram Kaïré signed the Artemis Accords on behalf of the Government of Senegal in the presence of Senior Bureau Official for African Affairs Jonathan Pratt and NASA Chief of Staff Brian Hughes.

    Ukraine Conflict News

  • 24 July 2025 - Daily Press Briefing by the Office of the Spokesperson for the Secretary-General United Nations 24 Jul 2025

    • Operation Iron Swords / Operation Al-Aqsa Flood | Operation Northern Arrows | Giddeon's Chariots

  • Hamas says submitted response to Gaza ceasefire proposal Press TV 24 Jul 2025 -- The Palestinian resistance group Hamas has confirmed it has submitted a formal response to the latest ceasefire proposal for Gaza.

  • Several settlers injured in car-ramming attack near Tulkarm in West Bank Press TV 24 Jul 2025 -- A car-ramming attack near the city of Tulkarm in the West Bank has injured eight Israeli settlers as Israeli forces seal off roads in the occupied territory.

  • Statement by UNICEF Regional Director for the Middle East and North Africa Edouard Beigbeder on the unconscionable deaths of children by starvation in the Gaza Strip UNICEF 24 Jul 2025
  • 'Famine silently begins to unfold' in Gaza, UNRWA chief says UN News 24 Jul 2025 -- "People in Gaza are neither dead nor alive, they are walking corpses." Philippe Lazzarini, Commissioner-General for the UN Relief and Works Agency for Palestinian Refugees (UNRWA), said that is what one of its workers told him on Thursday morning.
  • UNRWA Commissioner-General on Gaza: one in every five children is malnourished in Gaza City as cases increase every day UNRWA 24 Jul 2025
  • UN experts call for end to Israeli State and settler violence in the West Bank OHCHR 24 Jul 2025 -- UN experts* today expressed grave concern over systematic and ongoing violations by Israeli settlers and Israeli security forces against Palestinian peasants and rural workers in the occupied West Bank, including East Jerusalem.

  • Chairman Mast on France Recognizing 'State of Palestine' U.S. House of Representatives Committee on Foreign Affairs 24 Jul 2025

  • Iran condemns Israeli parliament's approval of annexing West Bank and Jordan Valley IRNA 24 Jul 2025 -- The Ministry of Foreign Affairs of the Islamic Republic of Iran has strongly condemned the Israeli Knesset's recent approval of a bill aimed at imposing the occupying regime's sovereignty over the West Bank and Jordan Valley.
  • Foreign Ministry spokesperson: Israeli bestial criminals must be reined in and prosecuted IRNA 24 Jul 2025 -- The spokesperson of the Ministry of Foreign Affairs says that Israeli criminals must be prosecuted for their atrocities.
  • Iran condemns Israeli Knesset vote on West Bank, Jordan Valley annexation Press TV 24 Jul 2025 -- Iran's Foreign Ministry has condemned the motion passed in the Israeli parliament (Knesset) that seeks to illegally annex the West Bank and Jordan Valley in breach of international law as the genocide in Gaza persists.

  • France to recognize Palestinian state at UN General Assembly, Macron announces Press TV 24 Jul 2025 -- France will officially recognize a Palestinian state this September at the United Nations General Assembly, President Emmanuel Macron has declared.
  • Joint Arab-Islamic Ministerial Committee Condemns the Israeli Knesset's Ratification of the Declaration Calling for the Imposition of So-called "Israeli Sovereignty" over the West Bank Organization of Islamic Cooperation (OIC) 24 Jul 2025 -- The Kingdom of Bahrain, the Arab Republic of Egypt, the Republic of Indonesia, the Hashemite Kingdom of Jordan, the Federal Republic of Nigeria, the State of Palestine, the State of Qatar, the Kingdom of Saudi Arabia, the Republic of Türkiye, the United Arab Emirates, the League of Arab States (LAS), and the Organization of Islamic Cooperation (OIC) vehemently condemn the recent ratification by the Israeli Knesset regarding the imposition of "Israeli sovereignty" over the occupied West Bank.
  • Foreign Ministry statement on the Israeli Parliament's adoption of a declaration on extending sovereignty to the territory of the West Bank Russia Ministry of Foreign Affairs 24 Jul 2025
  • Saudi Arabia, Arab and Islamic Countries Condemn Israeli Knesset's Declaration on West Bank Sovereignty SPA 24 Jul 2025 -- The Kingdom of Saudi Arabia, Bahrain, Egypt, Indonesia, Jordan, Nigeria, Palestine, Qatar, Turkiye, the United Arab Emirates, the Arab League, and the Organization of Islamic Cooperation (OIC) strongly condemned the Israeli Knesset's approval of a declaration calling for the imposition of "Israeli sovereignty" over the occupied West Bank.
  • Saudi Arabia Strongly Condemns Israeli Knesset's Control Demand Over West Bank SPA 24 Jul 2025 -- The Ministry of Foreign Affairs has expressed the Kingdom of Saudi Arabia's strong condemnation of the Israeli Knesset's demand to impose control over the occupied West Bank and the occupied Palestinian Territories, deeming it a blatant violation of international law and United Nations resolutions
  • Spain condemns Israeli Knesset's resolution on Occupied Palestinian Territories Spain MFA 24 Jul 2025
  • PM statement on Gaza: 24 July 2025 Gov.UK 24 Jul 2025

  • Yemen calls for unified stance of Islamic and Arab governments in confronting Israel IRNA 24 Jul 2025 -- Mahdi Al-Mashat, the head of the Supreme Political Council of Yemen, called on Islamic and Arab nations and governments to adopt a strong and unified position and take effective and immediate action to confront the pervasive threat of the Israeli regime and save the lives of the residents of the Gaza Strip.
  • Houthi: Zionist entity an enemy of all races, threat to every nation Press TV 24 Jul 2025 -- The leader of Yemen's Ansarullah movement, Abdul-Malik al-Houthi, has strongly condemned Israeli military aggression in Gaza and criticized Arab and Muslim countries for their silence and inaction regarding the situation in the Palestinian territories.

    • Other Conflicts

  • Global collaboration grows to address crises in Gaza, Sudan, Afghanistan UN News 24 Jul 2025 -- Amid growing geopolitical turbulence, the United Nations is deepening its collaboration with the Organization of Islamic Cooperation (OIC) to help address some of the world's most complex and protracted conflicts, from Gaza and Sudan to Myanmar and Afghanistan.
  • UN-Islamic Bloc Ties Vital, Security Council Told, as It Adopts Presidential Statement UN Security Council 24 Jul 2025 -- Amid growing geopolitical complexity, cooperation between the United Nations and the Organization of Islamic Cooperation (OIC) remains indispensable for maintaining international peace and security, the Security Council heard today, as it agreed on a presidential statement acknowledging the valuable role of the bloc mostly representing Muslim-majority countries.

  • Today's top news: Occupied Palestinian Territory, Sudan, Ukraine, Syria UNOCHA 24 Jul 2025

  • Afghanistan: UN report documents human rights violations of forced returnees OHCHR 24 Jul 2025 -- A UN report published today documents the cases of individuals involuntarily returned to Afghanistan who have experienced serious human rights violations on the basis of their specific profiles.
  • Torture, threats and arbitrary arrests: UN warns of 'serious abuses' against Afghans forced to return UN News 24 Jul 2025 -- Afghans returning to their country face "serious violations" of their human rights committed by the Taliban de facto authorities, according to a United Nations report published on Thursday amid mass deportation campaigns from Iran and Pakistan.
  • UK Data Leak Spurs Costly Afghan Resettlement And Security Fears RFE/RL 24 Jul 2025 -- Mirwais and family have left their home in Kabul and have been constantly changing their location since 2022, when a group of armed men shot dead his elder brother, a British-trained police officer.

  • Pakistan: Widespread impunity for violence and discrimination against minorities must end, warn UN experts OHCHR 24 Jul 2025 -- UN human rights experts* today called on the government of Pakistan to take concrete measures to prevent extrajudicial killings, arbitrary arrests and attacks against places of worship and cemeteries amid ongoing violence and discrimination against religious minorities, including the Ahmadi community.

  • On 23 July 2025, Security Forces conducted an intelligence based operation in Mastung District, on reported presence of terrorists belonging to Indian Proxy, Fitna al Hindustan. ISPR Pakistan 24 Jul 2025

  • Syria: Statement by the High Representative on behalf of the European Union on the situation EC/CEU 24 Jul 2025 -- The European Union strongly condemns the violence that resulted in hundreds of victims last week in Southern Syria, and especially attacks reportedly perpetrated by several armed groups against civilians.
  • UN experts welcome lifting of sanctions to rebuild Syria OHCHR 24 Jul 2025 -- UN experts* today welcomed the recent lifting of sanctions on Syria by the United States, European Union, United Kingdom and Switzerland, after more than 14 years of severe and widespread human rights impacts across the country.
  • Syria, Saudi Arabia to Sign Agreements Worth $6Bln Sputnik 24 Jul 2025 -- Damascus and Riyadh have agreed to sign more than 40 agreements worth $6 billion that will affect all industries, Syrian Information Minister Hamza al-Mustafa said.

  • Deadly clashes erupt along border of Cambodia, Thailand Radio Free Asia 24 Jul 2025 -- The neighboring countries trade blame after skirmishes in disputed areas kill at least 12 people and injure dozens of others.
  • On the Thailand-Cambodia Border Conflict US Dept. of State 24 Jul 2025 -- "The United States is gravely concerned by reports of the escalating fighting along the Thailand-Cambodia border."
  • China calls on Thailand, Cambodia to resolve conflict through dialogue Global Times 24 Jul 2025 -- A Chinese Foreign Ministry spokesperson expressed deep concern over current developments between Thailand and Cambodia on Thursday, urging both parties to resolve the issue through dialogue and negotiation after fresh clashes broke out along their border.
  • China calls for dialogue, consultation after Thailand-Cambodia border clash Global Times 24 Jul 2025 -- At a press conference on Thursday, Chinese Foreign Ministry spokesperson Guo Jiakun, when responding to a related question related to the Thailand-Cambodia border clash, stated that Thailand and Cambodia are both friendly neighbors of China and important members of ASEAN. Maintaining good-neighborly relations and properly handling differences serve the fundamental and long-term interests of both sides.
  • Thailand-Cambodia conflict intensifies with heavy weapons; Chinese expert warns of further escalation Global Times 24 Jul 2025 -- The Thailand-Cambodia conflict on Thursday escalated with the deployment of heavy weapons such as fighter jets and rocket artillery following landmine incidents along the disputed border, according to media reports. A Chinese military affairs expert said the clashes were intensifying, and warned of further escalation between China's two friendly neighbors.
  • The Military Clashes between the Kingdom of Cambodia and the Kingdom of Thailand (Statement by Foreign Minister IWAYA Takeshi) Ministry of Foreign Affairs of Japan 24 Jul 2025
  • PH calls for calm amid deadly Thai-Cambodia border clash Philippine News Agency 24 Jul 2025 -- The Philippine government urged for de-escalation following a violent military border clash between Thailand and Cambodia that reportedly killed at least 12 people on Thursday.
  • Foreign Ministry Spokeswoman Maria Zakharova's answer to media question on Thai-Cambodian border conflict Russia Ministry of Foreign Affairs 24 Jul 2025
  • Thailand And Cambodia On Brink Of War: What You Need To Know So Far Sputnik 24 Jul 2025 -- Deadly clashes have erupted between Thai and Cambodian forces in a long-disputed border zone. What do we know so far about the July 24 escalation?
  • Border clashes between Cambodia and Thailand leave 12 dead Press TV 24 Jul 2025 -- At least 12 people have been killed and 35 others wounded amid renewed armed clashes between Thai and Cambodian forces in the contested Emerald Triangle, a volatile region where the borders of Thailand, Cambodia, and Laos intersect.
  • Meeting between State Minister for Foreign Affairs MIYAJI and H.E. Mr. Maris Sangiampongsa, Minister of Foreign Affairs of the Kingdom of Thailand Ministry of Foreign Affairs of Japan 24 Jul 2025
  • Cambodia downgrades diplomatic relations with Thailand to 'the lowest level' amid cross-fire Global Times 24 Jul 2025 -- The Thai Army said on Thursday that a clash happened at the border areas with Cambodia after the Cambodian side first opened fire, the Xinhua News Agency reported.

  • ICC Trial Chamber V finds Alfred Yekatom and Patrice-Edouard Ngaïssona guilty of war crimes and crimes against humanity and sentences them to 15 and 12 years of imprisonment ICC 24 Jul 2025 -- Today, 24 July 2025, Trial Chamber V of the International Criminal Court ("ICC" or "Court") issued its verdict in the case The Prosecutor v. Alfred Yekatom and Patrice-Edouard Ngaïssona.
  • Statement of the ICC Office of the Prosecutor on the conviction of Messrs. Yekatom and Ngaïssona ICC 24 Jul 2025 -- The Office of the Prosecutor of the International Criminal Court welcomes today's judgment issued by Trial Chamber V convicting two members of the Anti-Balaka armed group, Mr Alfred Yekatom, former zone commander, and Mr Patrice-Edouard Ngaïssona, National General Coordinator.
  • Two ex-militia leaders in Central African Republic sentenced for war crimes, crimes against humanity UN News 24 Jul 2025 -- Two former leaders of the predominantly Christian Anti-Balaka militia in the Central African Republic have been convicted of multiple war crimes and crimes against humanity by the International Criminal Court (ICC).

News Reports

  • Telephone conversation with President of Uzbekistan Shavkat Mirziyoyev President of Russia 24 Jul 2025

  • Foreign Ministry Spokesperson Guo Jiakun's Regular Press Conference on July 24, 2025 PRC MofA 24 Jul 2025
  • Xi Jinping Meets with President of the European Council António Costa and President of the European Commission Ursula von der Leyen PRC MofA 24 Jul 2025 -- On the morning of July 24, 2025, President Xi Jinping met with President of the European Council António Costa and President of the European Commission Ursula von der Leyen, who are in China for the 25th China-EU Summit, at the Great Hall of the People in Beijing.
  • Wang Yi Meets with Pakistani Chief of Army Staff Syed Asim Munir PRC MofA 24 Jul 2025 -- On July 24, 2025, Member of the Political Bureau of the CPC Central Committee and Director of the Office of the Central Commission for Foreign Affairs Wang Yi met with Pakistani Chief of Army Staff Syed Asim Munir in Beijing.
  • Remarks by President António Costa at the joint press conference with President von der Leyen following the EU-China Summit in Beijing EC/CEU 24 Jul 2025
  • Opening remarks by President António Costa at the meeting with President of China Xi Jinping EC/CEU 24 Jul 2025
  • Opening remarks by President António Costa at the meeting with Premier of China Li Qiang EC/CEU 24 Jul 2025
  • Statement by President von der Leyen with President Costa following the EU-China Summit European Commission 24 Jul 2025
  • Opening remarks by President von der Leyen with President Costa and Premier Li Qiang at the EU-China Summit European Commission 24 Jul 2025
  • Opening remarks by President von der Leyen with President Costa and Xi Jinping, President of the People's Republic of China European Commission 24 Jul 2025-
  • 25th EU-China summit - EU press release European Commission 24 Jul 2025
  • Xi meets EU leaders in Beijing Global Times 24 Jul 2025 -- Chinese President Xi Jinping on Thursday called on China and the European Union (EU) to provide more stability and certainty for the world through steady and sound China-EU relations, according to the Xinhua News Agency.
  • China, EU should uphold openness and cooperation, properly manage differences: Xi Global Times 24 Jul 2025 -- Chinese President Xi Jinping on Thursday said that China and the European Union should uphold openness and cooperation, and properly manage differences.
  • Xi calls on China, EU to provide more stability, certainty for world through steady, sound bilateral relations PRC MND 24 Jul 2025 -- Chinese President Xi Jinping on Thursday called on China and the European Union (EU) to provide more stability and certainty for the world through steady and sound China-EU relations.
  • Xi calls on China, EU to provide more stability, certainty for world through steady, sound bilateral relations Global Times 24 Jul 2025 -- Chinese President Xi Jinping on Thursday called on China and the European Union (EU) to provide more stability and certainty for the world through steady and sound China-EU relations.
  • FM responds to whether China-EU leaders discuss rare earth exports issue Global Times 24 Jul 2025 -- In response to a media inquiry regarding the EU's concerns about China's restrictions on the export of rare earth metals and magnets, and whether China-EU leaders discussed the issue today, Chinese Foreign Ministry spokesperson Guo Jiakun stated on Thursday that China's policies are in line with international practices. He added that China is willing to strengthen dialogue and cooperation on export controls with relevant countries and regions to jointly safeguard the stability and security of global production and supply chains.
  • Chinese Embassy in the UK urges Europe to respect market economy after latest 'overcapacity' claim Global Times 24 Jul 2025 -- The Chinese Embassy in the UK on Wednesday rejected fresh EU allegations that Beijing's industrial subsidies and "excess capacity" are distorting global markets and widening the EU-China trade deficit, urging the EU to avoid the approach of politicizing economic issues and overstretching security concepts in normal business cooperation.
  • Xi meets European Council president, European Commission president in Beijing Global Times 24 Jul 2025 -- Chinese President Xi Jinping met with President of the European Council Antonio Costa and President of the European Commission Ursula von der Leyen in Beijing on Thursday, according to the Xinhua News Agency.
  • Chinese Embassy in UK responds to EU concerns over China's rare-earth exports control measures affecting shipments Global Times 24 Jul 2025 -- In response to a media inquiry about European companies' concerns that China's export control measures on rare earths might affect shipments to the EU, the Chinese Embassy in the UK said on its WeChat account on Thursday that it is a sovereign right and international responsibility for any country to impose necessary regulations on dual-use items - goods that can serve both civilian and military purposes.
  • China plans to venture farther into deep space People's Daily 24 Jul 2025 -- China has achieved remarkable accomplishments in the field of deep space exploration and is advancing its next ambitious missions, such as a Mars sample-return adventure and a Jupiter system expedition.

  • Ex-intel chief calls on U.S. to help Taiwan upgrade classified clearance system CNA 24 Jul 2025 -- A former U.S. naval intelligence chief on Wednesday called on Washington to help Taiwan upgrade its classified clearance system amid growing threats from China.
  • China appreciates South African moves to relocate, rename Taiwan 'offices': FM Global Times 24 Jul 2025 -- The South African government has issued a Government Gazette officially withdrawing its diplomatic recognition of Taiwan's representative office in Pretoria. It has also called on the Taiwan authorities to relocate the institution to Johannesburg and rename the "Taipei Liaison Office" in Cape Town as the "Taipei Commercial Office."

  • Arizona Woman Sentenced for $17M Information Technology Worker Fraud Scheme that Generated Revenue for North Korea US Dept. of Justice 24 Jul 2025 -- An Arizona woman was sentenced today to 102 months in prison for her role in a fraudulent scheme that assisted North Korean Information Technology (IT) workers posing as U.S. citizens and residents with obtaining remote IT positions at more than 300 U.S. companies.
  • United States Disrupts North Korea Revenue Generation, Offering Rewards of Up to $15 Million US Dept. of State 24 Jul 2025 -- Today, the Departments of State, Justice, and the Treasury are executing coordinated, decisive actions to keep Americans safe from North Korea's malicious and illicit revenue generation schemes.
  • Treasury Sanctions Clandestine IT Worker Network Funding the DPRK's Weapons Programs US Treasury 24 Jul 2025 -- Today, the U.S. Department of the Treasury's Office of Foreign Assets Control (OFAC) sanctioned the Korea Sobaeksu Trading Company and three associated individuals—Kim Se Un, Jo Kyong Hun, and Myong Chol Min—for their involvement in the evasion of U.S. and United Nations (UN) sanctions and efforts to generate revenue clandestinely for the Democratic People's Republic of Korea (DPRK) government, including through fraudulent information technology (IT) worker schemes.

  • Iran will not relinquish its right to nuclear enrichment: Araqchi IRNA 24 Jul 2025 -- Foreign Minister Abbas Araqchi has stressed that Iran will not back down from its nuclear rights and enrichment activities.
  • Enrichment will continue, Iran affirms ahead of talks with Europe Press TV 24 Jul 2025 -- Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi has reiterated Iran's unwavering stance on its nuclear program, emphasizing the nation's right to pursue peaceful nuclear energy, including uranium enrichment.
  • A.E.O.I. spokesperson says Iran's nuclear industry is domestic and will thrive again IRNA 24 Jul 2025 -- The spokesperson for the Atomic Energy Organization of Iran (A.E.O.I.) says that the nuclear industry of Iran is a domestically rooted one and will once again flourish.
  • Iran's nuclear industry deeply rooted, poised to rebloom: AEOI Press TV 24 Jul 2025 -- The spokesman for the Atomic Energy Organization of Iran (AEOI) has reiterated that Iran's nuclear industry has its roots in the country, making it resilient to attacks and external pressure.
  • Iran mission slams 'misleading' EU-Japan statement on US-Israeli aggression Press TV 24 Jul 2025 -- The Iranian Embassy in Tokyo has strongly condemned a joint EU-Japan statement regarding US-Israeli actions against Iran's peaceful nuclear facilities and civil infrastructure, labeling it as "biased and misleading."
  • FM: Iran resolute in safeguarding its rights in response to Israeli-US war Press TV 24 Jul 2025 -- Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi has underlined Iran's resolve to safeguard its rights in response to last month's unprovoked war by the Israeli regime and the United States.

  • Prime Minister meets Prime Minister of the United Kingdom (July 24, 2025) India MEA 24 Jul 2025 -- During his official visit to the United Kingdom from 23-24 July 2025, Prime Minister Shri Narendra Modi met today with the Prime Minister of the United Kingdom, Rt. Hon. Sir Keir Starmer. On arrival at the UK Prime Minister's country residence at Chequers in Buckinghamshire, he was warmly received by Prime Minister Starmer.
  • PM meeting with Prime Minister Modi of India: 24 July 2025 Gov.UK 24 Jul 2025
  • PARLIAMENT QUESTION: COMPETENCY OF ANDHRA PRADESH IN SPACE TECHNOLOGY India PIB 24 Jul 2025 -- IN-SPACe has approached various Indian states, including Andhra Pradesh seeking their interest in establishing space manufacturing cluster
  • PARLIAMENT QUESTION: SATELLITES LAUNCHED BY ISRO India PIB 24 Jul 2025 -- The following is the number of satellites realized by ISRO since 2020 along with its objectives:>Sl
  • PARLIAMENT QUESTION: STRATEGIES TO EXPAND INDIA'S SHARE IN THE GLOBAL SPACE ECONOMY India PIB 24 Jul 2025 -- IN-SPACe has approached various Indian states, including Andhra Pradesh seeking their interest in establishing space manufacturing cluster.

  • Transcript of the Weekly Press Briefing by the Spokesperson, Thursday July 24, 2025 Pakistan Ministry of Foreign Affairs 24 Jul 2025

  • Japan-ROK Foreign Ministers' Telephone Talks Ministry of Foreign Affairs of Japan 24 Jul 2025

  • EU Statement - Statement on existing international legal and other normative frameworks concerning threats arising from State behaviours with respect to outer space European External Action Service (EEAS) 24 Jul 2025
  • EU Statement - Open Ended Working Group on the Prevention of an Arms Race in Outer Space in all its aspects European External Action Service (EEAS) 24 Jul 2025

  • Türk hails landmark ICJ ruling affirming States' human rights obligations with respect to climate change OHCHR 24 Jul 2025 -- The Advisory Opinion issued by the International Court of Justice (ICJ) on States' obligations regarding climate change is a sweeping victory for all those who are fighting to protect a safe climate and planet for all humanity, UN Human Rights Chief Volker Türk said today.
  • Statement attributable to the Spokesperson for the Secretary-General - on the Joint Press Statement on Climate Change at the China-EU Summit UN Secretary-General 24 Jul 2025

  • Ranking Member Shaheen, Senators Scott, Kaine, Curtis Statement of Support for the Venezuelan People US Senate Foreign Relations Committee 24 Jul 2025 -- Today, U.S. Senators Jeanne Shaheen (D-NH), Ranking Member of the Senate Foreign Relations Committee, Rick Scott (R-FL), Tim Kaine (D-VA) and John Curtis (R-UT), released the following statement of support for the Venezuelan people:

  • Moldova Gov't Eliminates Political Rivals by Denying Pobeda Bloc Election Registration - Russia Sputnik 24 Jul 2025 -- The Moldovan government is trying to eliminate its rivals from the outset by denying the opposition Pobeda (Victory) bloc registration for the upcoming elections, Russian Foreign Ministry spokeswoman Maria Zakharova said on Thursday.
  • Moldovan Opposition Leader Says Sale of Giurgiulesti Port 'Betrayal of National Interests' Sputnik 24 Jul 2025 -- The leader of the opposition Future of Moldova party and former prime minister, Vasile Tarlev, condemned on Thursday the sale of the country's only seaport, Giurgiulesti, to a Romanian company as a "betrayal of national interests."

  • World News in Brief: Thailand-Cambodia border hostilities, humanitarian efforts in Syria and attacks across Ukraine UN News 24 Jul 2025 -- In a dramatic escalation of a long-standing border dispute, a deadly exchange of fire between Thailand and Cambodia killed at least 11 civilians and a soldier on Thursday, according to news reports. Both states are claiming the other side fired first.
